sentence_reviews.text: Probabilistic programming expresses generative assumptions as executable programs and delegates posterior inference to sampling, variational, or differentiable machinery.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML covers probabilistic models, Bayesian inference, MCMC, and variational inference; Deep Learning covers differentiable computation. Packet lacks a dedicated probabilistic-programming source.
sentence_reviews.notes: The definition is standard and defensible from PML inference topics, but not directly verified by a cited PPL-specific source.

sentence_reviews.text: The role of Probabilistic & differentiable programming is to connect Programs, ASTs, bytecode & intermediate representations, Bayesian & approximate inference to downstream capabilities including Scientific ML, simulators & digital twins, Knowledge graphs, semantic search & relational reasoning.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML supports Bayesian/approximate inference; packet sources do not directly support AST/IR, digital twins, or knowledge graph downstream claims.
sentence_reviews.notes: Bayesian inference connection is supported; most graph-role/downstream wording is not directly evidenced.

sentence_reviews.text: substrate for Scientific ML, simulators & digital twins
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML supports probabilistic modeling and inference but not the exact scientific-ML/simulator/digital-twin downstream claim in the inspected packet.
sentence_reviews.notes: PPL is plausibly useful for simulators/scientific modeling, but direct evidence is absent.

sentence_reviews.text: substrate for Knowledge graphs, semantic search & relational reasoning
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML inference topics do not directly support knowledge graphs, semantic search, or relational reasoning as downstream of PPL.
sentence_reviews.notes: The packet needs a relational/probabilistic logic or knowledge-graph source for this claim.

sentence_reviews.text: Its inductive bias, data demand, optimization behavior, and compute profile can make it unsuitable outside the regimes where it was validated. Its direct dependencies include Programs, ASTs, bytecode & intermediate representations, Bayesian & approximate inference.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML supports inference dependencies; no cited source directly states the generic limitation or program/AST/IR dependency.
sentence_reviews.notes: Bayesian inference dependency is supported; limitation and AST/IR wording are only partially supported.

sentence_reviews.text: Probabilistic & differentiable programming consumes, emits, or organizes information using Programs, ASTs, bytecode & intermediate representations.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML covers probabilistic models/inference but the packet lacks a specific source on program representations or AST/IR for probabilistic programming.
sentence_reviews.notes: Executable programs are inherent to probabilistic programming, but AST/bytecode/IR is not directly supported by packet evidence.

sentence_reviews.text: Probabilistic & differentiable programming is learned, selected, or evaluated using the objective family Bayesian & approximate inference.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML covers Bayesian inference, approximate inference, MCMC, and variational methods.
sentence_reviews.notes: Posterior inference is central to probabilistic programming.

sentence_reviews.text: Probabilistic & differentiable programming is assembled in part from the mechanism or model component Reparameterization & differentiable sampling.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Deep Learning and PML support differentiable computation, reparameterization, and variational inference generally.
sentence_reviews.notes: Common in differentiable PPL/variational methods, but not required for all probabilistic programming.

sentence_reviews.text: Probabilistic & differentiable programming is assembled in part from the mechanism or model component Constraint propagation, logic & program execution.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML supports probabilistic inference; AIMA supports logic/constraint/program-like reasoning, but no inspected source directly ties constraint propagation to PPL.
sentence_reviews.notes: Program execution is relevant; constraint propagation/logic is not generally required.

edge_reviews.notes: Programs are inherent to the paradigm, but AST/bytecode/IR details are not directly cited; core strength is plausible but under-sourced.
Bayesian and approximate posterior inference directly supports a core uses edge.
Differentiable sampling is common for differentiable/variational implementations, but not universal, so common strength is appropriate.
Program execution is relevant, but constraint propagation/logic is not generally evidenced for PPL; relationship is only partially supported.
